title: Introducing GPT-5.4 mini and nano
summary: GPT-5.4 mini and nano are smaller, faster versions of GPT-5.4 optimized for coding, tool use, multimodal reasoning, and high-volume API and sub-agent workloads.
title_es: OpenAI lanza GPT-5.4 mini y nano para APIs y agentes
bajada: OpenAI presentó versiones más compactas y rápidas de GPT-5.4, optimizadas para coding, uso de herramientas y cargas de trabajo de alto volumen. Estas versiones buscan democratizar el acceso a modelos poderosos para desarrolladores con restricciones de latencia y costo.
bajada_en: OpenAI released smaller and faster versions of GPT-5.4 optimized for coding, tool use, and high-volume workloads. These models aim to democratize access to powerful AI for developers facing latency and cost constraints.

title: Mistral bets on ‘build-your-own AI’ as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
summary: Mistral Forge lets enterprises train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning and retrieval-based approaches.
title_es: Mistral Forge: entrenar modelos IA propios sin dependencias externas
bajada: Mistral lanzó Mistral Forge, una plataforma que permite a empresas entrenar modelos IA personalizados desde cero sobre sus propios datos, sin depender de fine-tuning o retrieval. Este movimiento desafía directamente el enfoque de competidores como OpenAI y Anthropic.
bajada_en: Mistral launched Mistral Forge, enabling enterprises to train custom AI models from scratch on proprietary data without relying on fine-tuning. The platform directly challenges OpenAI and Anthropic's approaches.

title: The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says
summary: The Pentagon is discussing plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their models on classified data, MIT Technology Review has learned.  AI models like Anthropic’s Claude are already used to answer questions in classified settings; applications include analyzing targets in Iran. But allowing models to train on…
title_es: Pentágono planea entrenar modelos IA con datos clasificados
bajada: El Pentágono está discutiendo planes para crear ambientes seguros donde compañías de IA entrenen versiones militares de sus modelos sobre datos clasificados. Esta iniciativa marca un cambio significativo en cómo agencias de defensa colaboran con empresas de IA.
bajada_en: The Pentagon is planning secure environments for AI companies to train military-specific model versions on classified data. This represents a significant shift in how defense agencies collaborate with AI firms.

title: Nemotron 3 Nano 4B: A Compact Hybrid Model for Efficient Local AI
title_es: NVIDIA lanza Nemotron 3 Nano 4B para IA local eficiente
bajada: NVIDIA presentó Nemotron 3 Nano 4B, un modelo compacto de 4 mil millones de parámetros diseñado para ejecución local y eficiente. Este modelo busca permitir despliegues de IA en dispositivos edge con recursos limitados.
bajada_en: NVIDIA unveiled Nemotron 3 Nano 4B, a compact 4B parameter model for efficient local AI deployment. The model targets edge devices and resource-constrained environments.

title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
summary: The latest open models and frameworks from NVIDIA bring together simulation, robot learning and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows.
title_es: NVIDIA facilita desarrollo de robots con IA y simulación
bajada: NVIDIA presentó frameworks y modelos abiertos que integran simulación, robot learning y compute embebido para acelerar workflows cloud-to-robot. Esta iniciativa simplifica el pipeline completo de desarrollo robótico con IA.
bajada_en: NVIDIA released open models and frameworks combining simulation, robot learning, and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows. The initiative streamlines end-to-end robotic AI development.

title: Justice Department Says Anthropic Can’t Be Trusted With Warfighting Systems
summary: In response to Anthropic’s lawsuit, the government said it lawfully penalized the company for trying to limit how its Claude AI models could be used by the military.
title_es: Justicia afirma que Anthropic no es confiable para sistemas bélicos
bajada: El Departamento de Justicia respondió a la demanda de Anthropic argumentando que penalizó legalmente a la empresa por intentar limitar el uso de Claude para aplicaciones militares. El conflicto refleja tensiones entre principios de seguridad corporativos y requerimientos de defensa nacional.
bajada_en: The Justice Department argued it lawfully penalized Anthropic for restricting Claude's military use. The conflict highlights tensions between corporate safety principles and national defense requirements.

title: Bringing the power of Personal Intelligence to more people
summary: Bubble that says "Personal Intelligence" with Google G, Google Photos logo, and Gmail logo around it
title_es: Google expande Personal Intelligence a más usuarios estadounidenses
bajada: Google está ampliando su feature Personal Intelligence, que permite a su asistente IA acceder al ecosistema Google (Gmail, Photos, etc.) para respuestas más personalizadas. La expansión lleva estas capacidades a todos los usuarios estadounidenses.
bajada_en: Google is expanding Personal Intelligence, allowing its AI assistant to access Gmail and Photos for more tailored responses. The feature is now rolling out to all US users.

title: langchain-ai /open-swe
summary:
Open-source framework for building your org's internal coding agent. Elite engineering orgs like Stripe, Ramp, and Coinbase are building their own internal coding agents — Slackbots, CLIs, and web apps that meet engineers where they already work. These agents are connected to internal systems with the right context, permissioning, and safety boundaries to operate with minimal human oversight. Open SWE is the open-source version of this pattern. Built on LangGraph and Deep Agents , it gives you the same architecture those companies built internally: cloud sandboxes, Slack and Linear invocation, subagent orchestration, and automatic PR creation — ready to customize for your own codebase and workflows. Note 💬 Read the announcement blog post here Architecture Open SWE makes the same core architectural decisions as the best internal coding agents. Here's how it maps to the patterns described in this overview of Stripe's Minions, Ramp's Inspect, and Coinbase's Cloudbot: 1. Agent Harness — Composed on Deep Agents Rather than forking an existing agent or building from scratch, Open SWE composes on the Deep Agents framework — similar to how Ramp built on top of OpenCode. This gives you an upgrade path (pull in upstream improvements) while letting you customize the orchestration, tools, and middleware for your org. create_deep_agent ( model = "anthropic:claude-opus-4-6" , system_prompt = construct_system_prompt ( repo_dir , ...), tools = [ http_request , fetch_url , commit_and_open_pr , linear_comment , slack_thread_reply ], backend = sandbox_backend , middleware = [ ToolErrorMiddleware (), check_message_queue_before_model , ...],
) 2. Sandbox — Isolated Cloud Environments Every task runs in its own isolated cloud sandbox — a remote Linux environment with full shell access. The repo is cloned in, the agent gets full permissions, and the blast radius of any mistake is fully contained. No production access, no confirmation prompts. Open SWE supports multiple sandbox providers o
title_es: Open SWE: framework open-source para agentes internos de código
bajada: LangChain lanzó Open SWE, un framework de código abierto para construir agentes de coding interno basados en patrones de empresas como Stripe y Coinbase. Incluye sandboxes en cloud, integración con Slack y orquestación automática de PRs.
bajada_en: LangChain released Open SWE, an open-source framework for building internal coding agents based on patterns from Stripe and Coinbase. Features include cloud sandboxes, Slack integration, and automatic PR creation.

title: Mar 12, 2026AnnouncementsAnthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network
summary: Announcements Anthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network Mar 12, 2026 We’re launching the Claude Partner Network, a program for partner organizations helping enterprises adopt Claude. We’re committing an initial $100 million to support our partners with training courses, dedicated technical support, and joint market development. Partners who join from today will get immediate access to a new technical certification and be eligible for investment. Anthropic is focused on ensuring that our AI model, Claude, serves the needs of businesses. To do this, we’ve partnered with a number of other companies. Notably, Claude is the only frontier AI model available on all three leading cloud providers: AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft. We also work with large management consultancies, professional services firms, specialist AI firms, and similar agencies. These organizations help our enterprise customers identify where Claude can provide the most value to their work, and then help them get started with our AI tools. Our partners act as trusted guides in what can feel like uncharted territory: navigating the deployment requirements, compliance, and change management necessary inside large organizations. Now, we’re doubling down our commitment to our partners, aiming to make it even easier for these organizations to support enterprises in adopting Claude. "Anthropic is the most committed AI company in the world to the partner ecosystem—and we're putting $100 million behind that this year to prove it. The certification, the co-investment, the dedicated team—this infrastructure is built so that any firm, at any scale, can build a Claude practice. Our partners are instrumental in getting enterprises from proof of concept to production with Claude, and we're making sure they have everything they need to do it."—Steve Corfield, Head of Global Business Development and Partnerships, Anthropic. Introducing the Claude Partner Network The Claude Partner Network provides
title_es: Anthropic invierte $100M en red de partners para Claude
bajada: Anthropic lanza el Claude Partner Network con una inversión inicial de $100 millones para ayudar a empresas a adoptar Claude. El programa ofrece capacitación, soporte técnico dedicado y desarrollo conjunto de mercado a partners certificados.
bajada_en: Anthropic launches the Claude Partner Network with an initial $100 million investment to help enterprises adopt Claude. The program offers training, dedicated technical support, and joint market development to certified partners.

title: Mar 11, 2026AnnouncementsIntroducing The Anthropic Institute
summary: Announcements Introducing The Anthropic Institute Mar 11, 2026 We’re launching The Anthropic Institute , a new effort to confront the most significant challenges that powerful AI will pose to our societies. The Anthropic Institute will draw on research from across Anthropic to provide information that other researchers and the public can use during our transition to a world containing much more powerful AI systems. In the five years since Anthropic began, AI progress has moved incredibly quickly. It took us two years to release our first commercial model, and just three more to develop models that can discover severe cybersecurity vulnerabilities , take on a wide range of real work , and even begin to accelerate the pace of AI development itself . We predict that far more dramatic progress will follow in the next two years. One of our company’s core convictions is that AI development is accelerating: that the improvements we make are compounding over time. Because of this, extremely powerful AI, like the kind our CEO Dario Amodei describes in Machines of Loving Grace , is coming far sooner than many think. If this is right, society is shortly going to need to confront many massive challenges . How will powerful AI systems reshape our jobs and economies? What kinds of opportunities for greater societal resilience will they give us? What kinds of threats will they magnify or introduce? What are the expressed “values” of AI systems and how will society help companies determine what the appropriate values are? And, if the recursive self-improvement of AI systems does begin to occur, who in the world should be made aware, and how should these systems be governed? The Anthropic Institute’s goal is to tell the world what we’re learning about these challenges as we build frontier AI systems, and to partner with external audiences to help address the risks we must confront. Whether our societies are able to do so will determine whether or not transformative AI delivers the r
title_es: Anthropic crea instituto para abordar desafíos de IA avanzada
bajada: Anthropic presenta The Anthropic Institute, un nuevo esfuerzo de investigación dedicado a resolver los retos más significativos que plantea la IA poderosa. La iniciativa busca proporcionar información basada en investigación para la transición hacia sistemas de IA más avanzados.
bajada_en: Anthropic announces The Anthropic Institute, a new research initiative focused on addressing the most significant challenges posed by powerful AI. The effort aims to provide research-based insights for the transition to more advanced AI systems.

bajada_en: Google engineers introduce Sashiko, an agentic AI tool for code review of the Linux kernel. The solution aims to automate and improve the review process for one of the most critical open-source projects.

bajada_en: After OpenAI's Instant Checkout fell short, Walmart embeds its Sparky chatbot directly into ChatGPT and Google Gemini. The shift reflects adjustments in agentic shopping strategies between both companies.

title: Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid

title_es: Patreon critica 'fair use' de empresas IA, pide pagar a creadores
bajada: El CEO de Patreon cuestiona los argumentos de fair use de compañías IA y exige compensación para creadores cuyos contenidos se usan en entrenamiento. La posición se suma a crecientes demandas de transparencia y pago por datos de entrenamiento.
bajada_en: Patreon's CEO challenges AI companies' fair use arguments and demands compensation for creators whose content is used in training. The stance joins growing demands for transparency and payment for training data.

title: Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business

title_es: División de networking de Nvidia genera $11B, rival potencial a chips
bajada: El negocio de networking de Nvidia generó $11 mil millones el último trimestre, rivalizando en escala con su negocio de chips. El crecimiento señala diversificación estratégica y oportunidades en infraestructura IA.
bajada_en: Nvidia's networking business generated $11 billion last quarter, now rivaling its chips division in scale. The growth signals strategic diversification and expanding AI infrastructure opportunities.

title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says smartphone apps will disappear as AI agents take their place

bajada: Carl Pei, CEO de Nothing, afirma que los agentes IA eventualmente reemplazarán las aplicaciones móviles tradicionales. La visión sugiere un cambio fundamental hacia interfaces basadas en intención en lugar de apps discretas.
bajada_en: Nothing CEO Carl Pei claims AI agents will eventually replace traditional mobile apps. The vision suggests a fundamental shift toward intent-based interfaces rather than discrete applications.

title: Federal cyber experts called Microsoft's cloud a "pile of shit," approved it anyway

bajada_en: Federal cybersecurity experts approved a Microsoft product despite years of security concerns. The incident highlights tensions between security standards and commercial decisions in critical infrastructure.

title: Two weeks ago, I posted here to see if people would be interested in an open-source local AI 3D model generator
summary: I posted a question about this idea here two weeks ago, kept working on it, and now I finally have a beta to show.It’s a local, open-source desktop app that generates 3D meshes from images.Right now it supports Hunyuan3D 2 Mini, and I’m already working on support for more open-source models. The app is built around an extension system to keep it modular.It’s still very early, so I’d genuinely love feedback from people here.I’m especially curious about a few things:What features would you care about most ?What kinds of file export extensions would actually be useful ?Which open-source models would you want supported first ?What would make something like this worth using for you?If anyone wants to check it out, here’s the GitHub :GitHub:https://github.com/lightningpixel/modlysubmitted by/u/Lightnig125[link][comments]
title_es: Desarrollador lanza generador 3D IA local y open-source
bajada: Un desarrollador presenta una aplicación desktop open-source que genera mallas 3D desde imágenes usando modelos locales. La herramienta soporta Hunyuan3D 2 Mini y está diseñada con arquitectura modular para expandir compatibilidad.
bajada_en: A developer releases an open-source desktop app that generates 3D meshes from images using local models. The tool supports Hunyuan3D 2 Mini and features modular architecture for expanding compatibility.

title: Rebel Audio is a new AI podcasting tool aimed at first-time creators
summary: Rebel Audio is a new all-in-one podcasting tool that allows creators to record podcasts, edit, clip content for social, and publish episodes, all without ever leaving the platform.

title: ChatGPT did not cure a dog’s cancer
summary: When an Australian tech entrepreneur with no background in biology or medicine said ChatGPT helped save his dog from cancer, the story spread with the kind of validation Big Tech has long craved: proof that AI will revolutionize medicine and take on one of its deadliest diseases. The reality, as usual, is more complicated. The […]
title_es: Verificado: ChatGPT no curó el cáncer del perro
bajada: Una investigación desmiente el relato viral de que ChatGPT ayudó a curar el cáncer de un perro. El caso ilustra la importancia de verificar afirmaciones sobre capacidades médicas de IA.
bajada_en: An investigation debunks the viral claim that ChatGPT helped cure a dog's cancer. The case illustrates the importance of verifying claims about AI's medical capabilities.

title: Gwen3.5-27b 8 bit vs 16 bit, 10 runs
summary: The Aider benchmark on Qwen3.5-27b with the four combinations of model weights at bf16, fp8 and KV cache at bf16 and fp8. Each benchmark was repeated 10 times. The variance observed is not statistical significant.FAQ:Why not do 100 runs? Each run is 1+ hours and I have other projects. The variance is already too little and even if we did observe some small thing with a lot of runs, it might not actually mean anything.Why the Aider benchmark? It sucks! Maybe - but I am researching for the specific purpose of agentic coding and I find the benchmark easy to use. The purpose is to find the impact of using a specific quantization, if any, not necessary to judge the model on the actual numbers.Can you test 4 bit, 5 bit etc? Yes, I am planning to.What did you set the context to? I did not set the context. It is not my benchmark. I am just a user.But I demand you tell me what the context is! Ok fine. The Aider benchmark is 224 tasks. On a typical run it used 2375980 prompt tokens and 613762 completion tokens. That works out to an average of 13300 tokens per task.That is not enough context for a good test! It might be if your use case is Aider. But anyway, I have an idea for how I might be able to artificially increase the context by filling in some garbage in the system prompt. I am going to try that.You are an idiot for claiming fp8 is as good as bf16! I am claiming nothing. I am just sharing my findings. I know I am personally probably going to choose fp8 based on this, but you do you. Also many might be restrained from using the full model, but still be interested in knowing how much damage they suffer from using a quant.This would be different if it was a knowledge based test. Maybe - I am considering finding a different benchmark to find out if that is the case. Although that is just because I am curious. My use case is agentic coding, so it wouldn't matter much to me.fp8 cache breaks down at longer context lengths! That is a claim worth researching. I will work on it.
title_es: Benchmarks cuantización Qwen3.5-27b: fp8 vs bf16 sin diferencia significativa
bajada: Un análisis de 10 ejecuciones del benchmark Aider en Qwen3.5-27b con diferentes cuantizaciones muestra variancia no significativa. Los resultados ayudan a developers de agentic coding a elegir configuraciones óptimas.
bajada_en: A 10-run analysis of the Aider benchmark on Qwen3.5-27b with different quantizations shows no statistically significant variance. Results help agentic coding developers choose optimal configurations.

title: The Gemini-powered features in Google Workspace that are worth using
summary: From summarizing emails, drafting content, organizing data, and tracking meetings, here are all the best Gemini features in Google Workspace.
title_es: Guía: mejores features de Gemini en Google Workspace
bajada: TechCrunch detalla las características más útiles de Gemini integradas en Google Workspace, desde resúmenes de emails hasta organización de datos. La guía ayuda a usuarios empresariales a maximizar valor de IA en sus flujos de trabajo.
bajada_en: TechCrunch details the most useful Gemini features integrated into Google Workspace, from email summaries to data organization. The guide helps enterprise users maximize AI value in their workflows.
